# NJ County Clustering Analysis

## Overview

This project applies K-Means clustering techniques to analyze socioeconomic patterns across New Jersey counties. The project was developed as part of a Data Mining course and deployed using Python CGI scripts on a Linux server environment.

The analysis focuses on identifying clusters of counties with similar characteristics using selected demographic and economic indicators.

## Features

* K-Means clustering implementation
* County-level socioeconomic analysis
* Automatically generated cluster visualizations
* Web-based Python CGI deployment
* Dynamic HTML output generation

## Technologies Used

* Python
* Scikit-learn
* Pandas
* Matplotlib
* MySQL
* CGI Programming
* Linux Server Environment

## Analysis Focus

The clustering analysis explored relationships between variables such as:

* Labor force participation
* Rent burden
* County-level socioeconomic indicators

## Purpose

The goal of this project was to explore how unsupervised machine learning techniques can identify meaningful patterns and similarities between counties based on socioeconomic data.
